channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Microsoft calls on Trump to loosen AI chip export restrictions
item.description: Microsoft is calling on the Trump administration to loosen Biden-era restrictions on artificial intelligence (AI) chip exports that it argues could undermine U.S. leadership on the technology.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Long-dormant Mac malware returns with advanced capabilities
item.description: Just two months into 2025 there have been numerous Mac malware threats targeting Apple laptops, ranging from infostealers to malicious software.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Windows 11 24H2 bug is confusing people by displaying half the interface in one language, and the remainder in another
item.description: I’ve seen some weird bugs in Windows 11, but a glitch that mixes up two languages in the operating system’s menus takes the cake.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: StarlingX bridges IPv4-IPv6 gap with dual-stack networking support
item.description: The Open Infrastructure Foundation is out with the release of StarlingX 10.0, a significant update to the open-source distributed cloud platform designed for IoT, 5G, O-RAN and edge computing applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: FBI says North Korean Lazarus hackers were behind $1.5 billion Bybit crypto hack
item.description: The FBI has accused the North Korean Lazarus Group of being behind the largest cryptocurrency theft in history.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: TI basada en productos: seis pasos clave para ejecutar el cambio
item.description: La mayoría de los departamentos de TI carecen de recursos y se ven obligados a debatir qué capacidades, mejoras y correcciones priorizar.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AMD’s Radeon RX 9070 XT price has supposedly leaked in MicroCenter's listing, and I honestly hope this is a joke
item.description: AMD's full reveal of the new Radeon RX 9000 series is just hours away, but MicroCenter's leaked listed prices could spell bad news for Team Red's competition against Nvidia's RTX 5000 series GPUs if they are legitimate.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hinge’s new feature wants to help trans and neurodivergent daters
item.description: Dating apps are a notoriously hostile space towards transgender users. If they list their transness in their profiles, they’re outing themselves to the world. If they don’t, matches could accuse them of catfishing. Picking the right time to tell matches can feel impossible.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Apple shareholders reject proposal to scrap company's diversity programs
item.description: Apple shareholders rebuffed an attempt to pressure the technology trendsetter into joining President Donald Trump's push to scrub corporate programs designed to diversify its workforce.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips that power artificial intelligence systems continued to grow.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At 10, USB-C still hasn’t lived up to its full potential
item.description: Slightly under 10 years ago, when I reviewed a new Apple MacBook, I devoted a surprising percentage of my wordage to its port. Yes, port—it had only one.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Las Vegas PD announce nation’s first fleet of police Tesla Cybertrucks
item.description: The Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department (LVMPD) announced it had been gifted the country’s first fleet of Tesla Cybertrucks for law enforcement use. During an address, LVMPD said an unnamed donor gifted the department at least 10 Cybertrucks to use for patrol and SWAT teams.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Cisco, Nvidia expand AI partnership to include Silicon One technology
item.description: Cisco and Nvidia have expanded their collaboration to support enterprise AI implementations by tying together Cisco’s Silicon One technology and Nvidia’s Ethernet networking platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Seniors Embracing Virtual Reality: A New World of Adventure and Therapy
item.description: Seniors are discovering the thrill of virtual reality, offering a new world of adventure and therapy that's changing lives one headset at a time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: MongoDB acquires Voyage AI to reduce hallucinations in AI applications
item.description: Database engine developer MongoDB has acquired Voyage AI in order to help enterprises reduce hallucinations in AI-powered applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A smarter approach to training AI models
item.description: Artificial intelligence, which was thought to be handily dominated by American labs and researchers, was thrown for a loop with DeepSeek’s R1. And yet, R1 is still subject to many of the same pitfalls as other models—we must make radical advances to move beyond the current limitations of AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
